title: Lysergic acid diethylamide induces increased signalling entropy in rats' prefrontal cortex.

assets.abstract: Psychedelic drugs are gaining attention from the scientific community as potential new compounds for the treatment of psychiatric diseases such as mood and substance use disorders. The 5-HT2A receptor has been identified as the main molecular target, and early studies pointed to an effect on the expression of neuroplasticity genes. Analysing RNA-seq data from the prefrontal cortex of rats chronically treated with lysergic acid diethylamide (LSD), we describe the psychedelic-induced rewiring of g

phase_mapping.bridge_rationale: Nonordinary — psychedelic decoupling, ego dissolution, DMN disruption, altered states.
